   132	
   133	The evaluator MUST NOT modify the worker branch or any protocol-owned mutable state other than the trial fields the submission writes (¬ß4.4) and the task it holds a claim on. In particular, the evaluator MUST NOT write to the trial's `completed_at`, `metrics`, `artifacts_uri`, `description`, or `status` directly; those writes are performed by the orchestrator when the submitted task reaches its terminal state (¬ß4.4, [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß4.3).
   134	
   135	### 4.3 Non-interference
   136	
   137	The evaluator reads the repository at the trial's worker-branch tip but MUST NOT push, rewrite, or delete that branch, and MUST NOT write to the canonical trial lineage. Any side effects of evaluation (test caches, build outputs, captured logs) MUST NOT be observable through any protocol-owned store, ref, or artifact location except via the evaluator's declared outputs (¬ß4.2).
   138	
   139	### 4.4 Submission
   140	
   141	The evaluator submits with:
   142	
   143	- `trial_id` ‚Äî the trial it evaluated.
   144	- `status` ‚Äî one of:
   145	  - `"success"` ‚Äî the trial ran and produced metrics.
   146	  - `"error"` ‚Äî the trial could not be evaluated for reasons attributable to the trial's own code (build failure, test failure, etc.). The evaluator MAY still include partial metrics.
   147	  - `"eval_error"` ‚Äî the evaluator itself failed for reasons unrelated to the trial's code (infrastructure fault, evaluator bug). While a fresh evaluate task MAY still be created for this trial, the trial's status MUST remain `"starting"`. If the orchestrator's retry policy is exhausted (or the operator abandons evaluation), the orchestrator MUST transition the trial's status to `"eval_error"`, making that status terminal for the trial ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß4.3).
   148	- `metrics` ‚Äî the metrics object described in ¬ß4.2. MAY be absent when `status == "eval_error"`.
   149	- `artifacts_uri` ‚Äî OPTIONAL. A URI the evaluator uploaded supporting artifacts to.
   150	
   151	On a `submitted ‚Üí completed` or `submitted ‚Üí failed` transition (per [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß4.3), the orchestrator MUST write the following trial fields atomically with the event:
   152	
   153	- `status` ‚Äî the trial status implied by the submission: `"success"` when the submission's `status == "success"`; `"error"` when the submission's `status == "error"`; unchanged from `"starting"` when the submission's `status == "eval_error"` (see ¬ß4.4 above for the terminal-retry case).
   154	- `metrics` ‚Äî set to the submission's `metrics` when `status ‚àà {"success", "error"}`. When `status == "eval_error"` the orchestrator MUST NOT write `metrics` on the trial; any submission-carried `metrics` is discarded.
   155	- `artifacts_uri` ‚Äî set to the submission's `artifacts_uri` when provided and `status ‚àà {"success", "error"}`. When `status == "eval_error"` the orchestrator MUST NOT write `artifacts_uri` on the trial; any submission-carried `artifacts_uri` is discarded. (An evaluator that wishes to retain diagnostic artifacts from a failed attempt MAY reference them in the `task.failed` event for that evaluate task; that channel is defined in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md).)
   156	- `completed_at` ‚Äî set to the time of the terminal trial transition, i.e. written exactly once, when the trial's status leaves `"starting"` (either on a `"success"`/`"error"` submission, or on the retry-exhausted `"eval_error"` transition). Intermediate `eval_error` submissions MUST NOT advance `completed_at`.
   157	
   158	On the retry-exhausted `"eval_error"` terminal transition itself, the orchestrator MUST NOT graft metrics or artifacts from any prior `eval_error` submission onto the trial; the trial's `metrics` and `artifacts_uri` fields remain unset. This keeps the trial object canonical: a trial either carries the outputs of a successful or code-level-failed evaluation, or it carries nothing.
   159	
   160	Resubmission is idempotent under the same rules as ¬ß3.4 and [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß4.2: identical normative fields (`trial_id`, `status`, `metrics`) MUST be accepted; inconsistent resubmission MUST be rejected. `artifacts_uri` is NOT part of equivalence ‚Äî the first submission's `artifacts_uri` is the committed one. (Earlier drafts of this section listed `artifacts_uri` as part of the equivalence formula; the ¬ß4.2 statement is canonical and this section now defers to it.)
   161	
   162	## 5. Integrator
   163	
   164	The integrator promotes successfully evaluated trials into the canonical trial lineage.
   165	
   166	The integrator's full contract ‚Äî the squash rule, the eval-manifest shape, conflict-resolution policy ‚Äî is deferred to [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md). This section pins only the boundary rules that every other role must honor.
   167	
   168	### 5.1 Exclusive authority
   169	
   170	The integrator is the **sole writer** of the canonical trial lineage (`trial/*` branches, `trial_commit_sha` fields on trials). Every other role MUST treat `trial/*` as read-only and MUST NOT set or modify a trial's `trial_commit_sha`.

1	# Conformance
     2	
     3	This chapter declares what conformance to EDEN protocol v0 means and how it is verified.
     4	
     5	## 1. What conformance is
     6	
     7	A v0-conforming IUT (implementation under test) implements the HTTP wire binding from [`07-wire-protocol.md`](07-wire-protocol.md) with the normative semantics of [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md), [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md), [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md), [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md), [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md), and [`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md).
     8	
     9	Conformance is verified by passing the conformance suite under [`conformance/`](../../conformance/) in this repo: a set of black-box scenarios that drive an IUT through the chapter-7 HTTP binding and assert MUSTs from those chapters. The suite is delivered in three increments ‚Äî **v1**, **v1+roles**, and **v1+roles+integrator** ‚Äî each adding a group of scenarios to the prior level. **An IUT MUST qualify its conformance claim with the level it passes** (e.g. "v1 conformant", "v1+roles+integrator conformant"); the term "v0 conformance" without a level qualifier is reserved for the final `v1+roles+integrator` level once it exists. Until then, an unqualified "v0 conformance" claim is not meaningful.
    10	
    11	The suite is one expression of the contract, not the only one. A future suite ‚Äî in a different language, against a different harness ‚Äî that drives the same chapter-7 endpoints and asserts the same MUSTs would be equally authoritative. The spec is the source of truth; the suite is one faithful implementation of "what would test that?"
    12	
    13	## 2. The IUT
    14	
    15	An IUT MUST:
    16	
    17	- Expose the chapter-7 HTTP binding ([`07-wire-protocol.md`](07-wire-protocol.md)) at a deployment-chosen base URL.
    18	- Accept an experiment configuration whose shape matches [`schemas/experiment-config.schema.json`](schemas/experiment-config.schema.json).
    19	
    20	Anything else ‚Äî language, deployment topology, persistence, auth scheme, scaling profile ‚Äî is a deployment choice. Further latitude is enumerated in [`07-wire-protocol.md`](07-wire-protocol.md) ¬ß11.
    21	
    22	## 3. Assertion vocabulary
    23	
    24	Every assertion in the v1 suite is keyed off a normative MUST in [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md), [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md), [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md), [`07-wire-protocol.md`](07-wire-protocol.md), or [`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md). The v1+roles suite extends this to include [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md). The v1+roles+integrator suite further extends to include [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md). The suite does NOT assert SHOULDs; SHOULDs are interop guidance, not interop contracts.
    25	
    26	When the spec evolves, the suite evolves with it. A change that removes a MUST or downgrades it to SHOULD MUST be accompanied by a matching scenario removal or rewrite.
    27	
    28	The suite intentionally does NOT certify two [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß1.3, ¬ß3.2 invariants: the atomicity-of-state-change-and-event-emission invariant and the claim-token unforgeability property. Black-box testing cannot prove the absence of a sufficiently-narrow window or the absence of a sufficiently-weak random source. The v1 suite asserts the testable consequences of these invariants (event presence, uniqueness across observed claims) and labels the regression-style tests as such.
    29	
    30	## 4. Conformance levels
    31	
    32	The v0 spec defines exactly one wire binding ([`07-wire-protocol.md`](07-wire-protocol.md)). The suite drives every IUT through it; transport-neutral semantic tests vs. per-binding wire tests cannot be meaningfully separated until a second binding exists. Levels are therefore distinguished by **scenario coverage**, not transport:
    33	
    34	- **v1** ‚Äî task-store and event-log MUSTs from [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md), [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md), [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md), [`07-wire-protocol.md`](07-wire-protocol.md), plus the storage MUSTs that the wire binding exposes from [`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) ¬ß1.1, ¬ß1.7. v1 does NOT cover the [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) role contracts or the [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) integrator atomicity ladder.
    35	- **v1+roles** ‚Äî adds [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) role-contract scenarios (per-role submission semantics, backpressure, idempotency).
    36	- **v1+roles+integrator** ‚Äî adds [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) ¬ß3.4 integrator scenarios (squash shape, eval-manifest shape, `work/*` access discipline, atomicity ladder under transport-indeterminate failures).
    37	
    38	A future spec lineage that introduces a second wire binding will at that point split the suite into transport-neutral semantic tests + per-binding wire tests, and chapter 9 will gain a `core` level claimable by IUTs implementing only the semantic layer. The marker structure in [`conformance/`](../../conformance/) anticipates that refactor but does not enable it in v0.
    39	
    40	## 5. Scenario index
    41	
    42	The v1 scenario groups, with their primary spec citations:
    43	
    44	| Group | Scope | Spec citations |
    45	|---|---|---|
    46	| Task lifecycle | Every legal/illegal transition. | [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß1, ¬ß2, ¬ß3, ¬ß4, ¬ß5 |
    47	| Claim tokens | Freshness, authorization, no-reclaim-while-claimed. | [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) ¬ß3.4; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß3, ¬ß5 |
    48	| Submit idempotency | Content-equivalent / divergent / post-terminal. | [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) ¬ß4.2, ¬ß4.4 |

    72	
    73	A v1 IUT does NOT need to ship a Python adapter. The reference suite's adapter shape ‚Äî under [`conformance/harness/adapter.py`](../../conformance/harness/adapter.py) ‚Äî is one convenience for IUTs that prefer to integrate with the reference Python harness; it is not a normative requirement. A non-Python implementor MAY:
    74	
    75	- Write a Python `IutAdapter` subclass that spawns their service.
    76	- Run the suite against an already-running IUT pointed at by an environment variable, via a thin Python adapter that simply returns an `IutHandle` to that URL.
    77	- Re-implement the suite in another language entirely, asserting the same MUSTs.
    78	
    79	The contract between an IUT and a conformance harness is the chapter-7 HTTP binding. Everything else is convenience.
    80	
    81	## 7. Reference posture
    82	
    83	The reference implementation in [`reference/`](../../reference/) demonstrates conformance at the highest currently-shipped level ‚Äî **v1** at the time chapter 9 first lands; **v1+roles** after chunk 11c; **v1+roles+integrator** after chunk 11d. The reference impl's conformance claim is always level-qualified per ¬ß1. CI gates the reference impl on every level the suite has shipped, via the `conformance` job. A reference-impl test failure that cannot be cited to a normative MUST is either a suite bug or a spec gap; the resolution is one of: amend the suite, amend the spec, fix the impl. The reference impl does NOT have authority to reinterpret the spec.

    71	    MUST reject this somewhere on the path from /submit to /accept;
    72	    the trial's terminal status MUST NOT become `success` from a
    73	    submission that omitted the required field. Where in the pipeline
    74	    the rejection surfaces is implementation-defined (¬ß9 latitude),
    75	    so the assertion checks the observable end-state rather than the
    76	    exact failing endpoint.
    77	    """
    78	    pid = _seed.create_proposal(wire_client)
    79	    _seed.mark_proposal_ready(wire_client, pid)
    80	    impl_tid = _seed.create_implement_task(wire_client, proposal_id=pid)
    81	    trial_id = _seed.create_trial(wire_client, proposal_id=pid, status="starting")
    82	    c = _seed.claim(wire_client, impl_tid)
    83	    # Wire-level submit without commit_sha. submit_implement always
    84	    # includes commit_sha on success, so build the request inline.
    85	    r = wire_client.post(
    86	        wire_client.tasks_path(impl_tid, "/submit"),
    87	        json={
    88	            "token": c["token"],
    89	            "payload": {
    90	                "kind": "implement",
    91	                "status": "success",
    92	                "trial_id": trial_id,
    93	            },
    94	        },
    95	    )
    96	    if r.status_code != 200:
    97	        # IUT rejected at submit ‚Äî conforming.
    98	        return
    99	    # IUT accepted at submit (commit_sha is optional in the wire
   100	    # shape). The orchestrator-side accept call MUST then reject and
   101	    # the trial MUST NOT terminalize as success.
   102	    accept = _seed.accept(wire_client, impl_tid)
   103	    if 200 <= accept.status_code < 300:
   104	        # Accept somehow succeeded ‚Äî the trial MUST NOT show success.
   105	        trial = _seed.read_trial(wire_client, trial_id)
   106	        assert trial["status"] != "success", (
   107	            "implement success without commit_sha must not produce a success trial"
   108	        )
   109	        return
   110	    # Accept rejected ‚Äî that's the validation_error path; the trial
   111	    # stays in starting until the orchestrator rejects the task with
   112	    # validation_error or reclaims it.
   113	    trial = _seed.read_trial(wire_client, trial_id)
   114	    assert trial["status"] != "success"

    51	    the pipeline the rejection surfaces is implementation-defined,
    52	    so the assertion checks the observable end-state.

    60	        trial_id=trial_id,
    61	        metrics={"score": 1.0, "undeclared_key": 99},
    62	    )
    63	    if r.status_code != 200:
    64	        # Rejected at submit ‚Äî conforming.
    65	        trial = _seed.read_trial(wire_client, trial_id)
    66	        assert trial["status"] != "success"
    67	        return
    68	    accept = _seed.accept(wire_client, eval_tid)
    69	    if 200 <= accept.status_code < 300:

    76	    trial = _seed.read_trial(wire_client, trial_id)

   281	def drive_to_starting_trial(
   282	    client: WireClient,
   283	    *,
   284	    proposal_id: str | None = None,
   285	    commit_sha: str = "1" * 40,
   286	) -> str:
   287	    """Drive a fresh proposal through implement-accept; trial is `starting` with commit_sha.
   288	
   289	    The trial is NOT in `success` yet ‚Äî that requires the evaluator
   290	    cycle to also complete. Returns trial_id.
   291	    """
   292	    pid = proposal_id or create_proposal(client)
   293	    mark_proposal_ready(client, pid)
   294	    impl_tid = create_implement_task(client, proposal_id=pid)
   295	    impl_claim = claim(client, impl_tid, worker_id="impl-worker")
   296	    trial_id = fresh_trial_id()
   297	    # Implementer creates the starting trial before submitting (chapter 3 ¬ß3.2 step 1).
   298	    create_trial(
   299	        client,
   300	        trial_id=trial_id,
   301	        proposal_id=pid,
   302	        status="starting",
   303	    )
   304	    r = submit_implement(
   305	        client,
   306	        impl_tid,
   307	        token=impl_claim["token"],
   308	        trial_id=trial_id,
   309	        commit_sha=commit_sha,
   310	    )
   311	    r.raise_for_status()
   312	    accept(client, impl_tid)
   313	    return trial_id

   316	def drive_to_success_trial(
   317	    client: WireClient,
   318	    *,
   319	    proposal_id: str | None = None,
   320	    commit_sha: str = "1" * 40,
   321	    metrics: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   322	) -> str:
   323	    """Drive a fresh proposal through full implement ‚Üí evaluate cycle.
   324	
   325	    Returns trial_id of a trial in `status="success"` (the evaluator
   326	    accept-step transitions the trial atomically per chapter 05 ¬ß2.2).
   327	    """
   328	    trial_id = drive_to_starting_trial(
   329	        client, proposal_id=proposal_id, commit_sha=commit_sha
   330	    )
   331	    eval_tid = create_evaluate_task(client, trial_id=trial_id)
   332	    eval_claim = claim(client, eval_tid, worker_id="eval-worker")
   333	    r = submit_evaluate(
   334	        client,
   335	        eval_tid,
   336	        token=eval_claim["token"],
   337	        trial_id=trial_id,
   338	        metrics=metrics,
   339	    )
   340	    r.raise_for_status()
   341	    accept(client, eval_tid)
   342	    return trial_id

1	# EDEN Conformance Suite
     2	
     3	Black-box test suite that any third-party implementation of an EDEN component can run against itself to prove conformance with the protocol specification.
     4	
     5	## Status
     6	
     7	**v1+roles shipped (Phase 11 chunk 11a + 11b + 11c).** The v1 suite covers the task-store + wire-binding subset: chapters 02 / 04 / 05 / 07 plus the storage MUSTs (chapter 08 ¬ß1.1, ¬ß1.7) the wire binding exposes. The v1+roles addendum (chunk 11c) covers chapter 03 ¬ß2.4 / ¬ß3.4 / ¬ß4.2 / ¬ß4.4 role-contract MUSTs across three new index groups (`Planner submission`, `Implementer submission`, `Evaluator submission`); 20 new scenarios bring the suite to 106 total. The chapter-06 integrator scenarios (chunk 11d) are out of v1+roles scope.
     8	
     9	See [`spec/v0/09-conformance.md`](../spec/v0/09-conformance.md) for the normative chapter, including the level taxonomy (`v1` / `v1+roles` / `v1+roles+integrator`).
    10	
    11	## Running the suite
    12	
    13	Against the reference implementation:
    14	
    15	```bash
    16	uv run pytest -q conformance/
    17	```
    18	
    19	Against a third-party IUT (whose adapter implements `conformance.harness.adapter.IutAdapter`):
    20	
    21	```bash
    22	uv run pytest -q conformance/ --iut-adapter=my_pkg.my_module:MyAdapter
    23	```
    24	
    25	Verify every scenario cites a real spec section:
    26	
    27	```bash
    28	uv run python conformance/src/conformance/tools/check_citations.py
    29	```
    30	
    31	## Layout
    32	
    33	- [`src/conformance/harness/`](src/conformance/harness/) ‚Äî `IutAdapter` Protocol, pytest plugin, thin httpx `WireClient`, event-log helpers, scenario seeding helpers.
    34	- [`src/conformance/adapters/reference/`](src/conformance/adapters/reference/) ‚Äî reference adapter that spawns `python -m eden_task_store_server` against in-memory storage.
    35	- [`src/conformance/_meta/`](src/conformance/_meta/) ‚Äî the `MisbehavingAdapter` + proxy used by the self-validation scenario.
    36	- [`src/conformance/tools/`](src/conformance/tools/) ‚Äî citation-check tool.
    37	- [`src/conformance/fixtures/`](src/conformance/fixtures/) ‚Äî minimal experiment-config used by the harness.
    38	- [`scenarios/`](scenarios/) ‚Äî the test files; each citation in a docstring's first line is verified by `tools/check_citations.py`.
    39	
    40	## Layer discipline
    41	
    42	- `src/conformance/harness/` and `scenarios/` MUST NOT import from `reference/` packages.
    43	- `src/conformance/adapters/reference/` is the only place where the reference impl is taught how to be a subject; it MAY import `eden_*` packages.
    44	- CI gates this directionality.
    45	
    46	## Adding a scenario
    47	
    48	Every test's docstring's first line MUST cite a normative MUST in the spec, in the format `spec/v0/<chapter>.md ¬ß<sec>`. CI fails if the citation can't be resolved or if a chapter-9 ¬ß5 scenario-index group has no citing test.
    49	
    50	For the full plan-writing pitfalls and conformance-discipline guidance, see [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md).

**Findings**
- Bug ‚Äî [test_implementer_submission.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/scenarios/test_implementer_submission.py:96): `test_success_without_commit_sha_must_not_complete_trial` treats any non-`200` submit response as ‚Äúconforming rejection‚Äù and any non-2xx `/accept` response as acceptable. That means a `500` can pass this conformance test. This contradicts the plan‚Äôs ‚Äúassert end-state, not endpoint‚Äù rule, which explicitly says to treat `4xx` as conforming rejection. Fix: require `400 <= status_code < 500` in the rejection branches and fail on `5xx`.

- Bug ‚Äî [test_evaluator_submission.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/scenarios/test_evaluator_submission.py:63), [test_evaluator_submission.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/scenarios/test_evaluator_submission.py:99): both evaluator end-state tests have the same bug. `r.status_code != 200` and `accept.status_code >= 400` allow server-side failures to satisfy the scenario as long as the trial never reaches `success`. The plan‚Äôs end-state-not-endpoint section does not permit that. Fix: tighten these branches to accept only `4xx` as conforming rejection.

- Risk ‚Äî [test_implementer_submission.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/scenarios/test_implementer_submission.py:180): the plan‚Äôs implementer `status=error` scenario said to assert three things: the trial becomes `error`, a `task.failed` event names the implement task, and no evaluate task is dispatched for that trial. The implemented test checks only the first and third. That is a plan-adherence gap and leaves the event side under-tested. Fix: add an explicit `task.failed` assertion for `impl_tid`.

- Risk ‚Äî [test_evaluator_submission.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/scenarios/test_evaluator_submission.py:115): `test_success_writes_trial_metrics_atomic` is named and documented as an atomicity test, but its assertions only check post-accept trial state plus presence of a `trial.succeeded` event. That does not actually exercise atomicity; it only proves both observables eventually exist. Fix: either strengthen it with the suite‚Äôs composite-commit style assertion, or narrow the name/docstring to say this scenario only covers positive trial-side writes.

- Risk ‚Äî [_seed.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/src/conformance/harness/_seed.py:304): `drive_to_starting_trial()` does not check whether the implementer `/accept` succeeded before returning a trial that callers assume is ready for evaluator scenarios. That makes the new evaluator tests less robust: a setup regression can surface later as a misleading downstream failure, or still satisfy some negative assertions. Fix: capture the accept response, require 2xx, and ideally verify the resulting trial has the expected `commit_sha` before returning.

**Overall assessment**

The chunk mostly matches the plan: the expected files are present, the spec/doc updates are aligned, and the targeted verification is green (`check_citations`, the 20 new scenarios, `ruff`, and `pyright` all pass). I would not sign off yet, though, because the most important negative conformance tests are too permissive about `5xx` failures, and one planned implementer assertion was dropped. Once those are tightened, the implementation looks solid.
